assumption_A.text: Crating-in and condition-checking the exhibit takes 4 install days at current staffing.

axis_params.irreducibility: op3 tests mounting finish q2 <= day 11. REVERSED-POLARITY: the correction EXTENDS crate-in. At 4 days (original): mounting finishes day 9<=11 (meets); at 8 days (corrected): day 13>11 (misses) — flips; ops (+,+,<=).

must_change: Crate-in finishes on install day 8.
Mounting finishes on install day 13.
Mounting does not complete by the install-day-11 press preview.
Crate-in plus the 3-day environmental-acclimation lead spans 11 install days.
must_persist: Mounting must complete by install day 11.
Lighting and labelling follow mounting.

not_A_evidence.text: Staffing revision (supersedes the crate estimate): an art-handling team shortfall extended crate-in to 8 install days.

question: Does mounting complete by the install-day-11 press preview?

sources.text: Install plan: at the Harlow Museum, a touring exhibit is crated-in, then mounted, then lit and labelled, in sequence, before a press-preview date. Crate-in starts on install day 0.
Crate estimate: crating-in and condition-checking the exhibit takes 4 install days at current staffing.
Preview plan: mounting always takes 5 install days after crate-in, lighting and labelling follow mounting, and mounting must complete by install day 11 to hold the press preview.
